name: aws-croniter

metadata.name: aws-croniter
metadata.summary: A Python utility for AWS cron expressions. Validate and parse AWS EventBridge cron expressions seamlessly.

documentation:
# AWS Croniter

AWS Croniter is a Python library that provides utilities for working with AWS cron expressions. It allows you to easily parse, manipulate, and validate cron expressions used in AWS services like CloudWatch and EventBridge.

## Features

- Parse AWS cron expressions
- Validate cron expressions
- Generate next execution times
- Convert cron expressions to human-readable format

## Installation

You can install AWS Croniter using pip:

```sh
pip install aws-croniter
```

## Usage

Here is a basic example of how to use AWS Croniter:

```python
from aws_croniter import Croniter

# Create a Croniter object with an AWS cron expression
cron = Croniter('cron(0 12 * * ? *)')

# Validate the cron expression
if cron.is_valid():
    print("The cron expression is valid.")

# Get the next execution time
next_time = cron.get_next()
print(f"The next execution time is: {next_time}")

# Convert to human-readable format
human_readable = cron.to_human_readable()
print(f"Human-readable format: {human_readable}")
```

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please read the [contributing guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md) first.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Contact

For any questions or suggestions, please open an issue or contact the maintainer at [email@example.com](mailto:email@example.com).

classes.name: aws_croniter.exceptions.AWSCronExpressionMinuteError
documentation: Exception raised for invalid minute values in an AWS cron expression.

documentation: [0]1-12 or JAN-DEC

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.python_to_aws_day_of_week
documentation: Convert Python day of week (Mon=0) to AWS day of week (Mon=2).

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.get_days_of_month_from_days_of_week
documentation: Get all days of the month that match the given days of the week.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.get_days_of_month_for_L
documentation: Get the last day of the month adjusted by a specific number of days.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.get_days_of_month_for_W
documentation:
Get the closest weekday for the specified day of the month.
Adjusts for weekends and ensures the date is within the month.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.is_weekday
documentation: Check if a specific day is a weekday (Mon-Fri).

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.DateUtils.is_day_in_month
documentation: Check if a specific day exists in a given month.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.TimeUtils.datetime_to_millisec
documentation: Convert a datetime object to milliseconds since epoch.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.SequenceUtils.array_find_first
documentation: Find the first element in a sequence that satisfies the given function.

methods.name: aws_croniter.utils.SequenceUtils.array_find_last
documentation: Find the last element in a sequence that satisfies the given function.

methods.name: aws_croniter.occurrence.Occurrence.next
documentation:
Generate the next occurrence after the current time.

```
param datetime_inclusive

If True, include the current time if it matches a valid execution.

return

The next occurrence as a datetime object.
```

methods.name: aws_croniter.occurrence.Occurrence.prev
documentation:
Generate the prev before the occurrence date value

```
param datetime_inclusive

If True, include the current time if it matches a valid execution.

return

The next occurrence as a datetime object.
```

methods.name: aws_croniter.awscron.AWSCron.__validate
documentation:
Validates these AWS EventBridge cron expressions, which are similar to, but not compatible with standard
unix cron expressions:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-create-rule-schedule.html#eb-cron-expressions

| Field        | Values          | Wildcards     |
| :----------: | :-------------: | :-----------: |
| Minute       | 0-59            | , - * /       |
| Hour         | 0-23            | , - * /       |
| Day-of-month | 1-31            | , - * ? / L W |
| Month        | 1-12 or JAN-DEC | , - * /       |
| Day-of-week  | 1-7 or SUN-SAT  | , - * ? L #   |
| Year         | 1970-2199       | , - * /       |

methods.name: aws_croniter.awscron.AWSCron.get_next_n_schedule
documentation:
Returns a list with the n next datetime(s) that match the aws cron expression from the provided start date.

```
param n

Int of the n next datetime(s)

param from_date

datetime with the start date

param cron

str of aws cron to be parsed

return

list of datetime objects
```

methods.name: aws_croniter.awscron.AWSCron.get_prev_n_schedule
documentation:
Returns a list with the n prev datetime(s) that match the aws cron expression
from the provided start date.

```
param n

Int of the n next datetime(s)

param from_date

datetime with the start date

param cron

str of aws cron to be parsed

return

list of datetime objects
```

methods.name: aws_croniter.awscron.AWSCron.get_all_schedule_bw_dates
documentation:
Get all datetimes from from_date to to_date matching the given cron expression.
If the cron expression matches either 'from_date' and/or 'to_date',
those times will be returned as well unless 'exclude_ends=True' is passed.

```
param from_date

datetime object from where the schedule will start with tzinfo in utc.

param to_date

datetime object to where the schedule will end with tzinfo in utc.

param cron

str of aws cron to be parsed

param exclude_ends

bool defaulted to False, to not exclude the end date

return

list of datetime objects
```
